steps.title: Use 5-hour periods
steps.content: A value that is $125\%$ greater is multiplied by $1+1.25=2.25$. Since the growth happens every $5$ hours, the exponent should count 5-hour periods: after $x$ hours, that is $x/5$ periods.

steps.title: Find the exponential pattern
steps.content: At $x=15$ hours, there have been $15/5=3$ growth periods and the mass is $637.88$ grams. Work backward to the starting value: $$a(2.25)^3=637.88$$ $$a=\frac{637.88}{(2.25)^3}\approx56.00$$ So the model is $$A=56.00(2.25)^{x/5}$$

steps.title: Confirm the choices
steps.content: The equations with base $1.25$ model $125\%$ of the old mass, not $125\%$ greater than the old mass. The equation with base $2.25$ and starting value $42.53$ has the right growth factor but the wrong initial mass. The correct equation has both model values, $56.00$ and $2.25$, so C is correct.
